summary: Fixed the live ops portal login flow by removing the invalid literal groups scope from the repo-managed oauth2-proxy configuration on nginx-lv3.

verification.observed: A live request to https://ops.example.com/oauth2/sign_in redirected to the Keycloak authorization endpoint with scope=openid+profile+email and no invalid groups scope.
The deployed /etc/lv3/oauth2-proxy/ops-portal.cfg on nginx-lv3 contains scope = "openid profile email".

verification.check: Ops portal auth proxy service state
verification.observed: systemctl is-active lv3-ops-portal-oauth2-proxy returned active on nginx-lv3 after the configuration update.

notes: The first public-edge converge applied the oauth2-proxy config change and restarted the auth proxy before later failing on missing locally generated static-site build directories in the worktree.
The live fix was verified externally through the public redirect and directly on nginx-lv3 through the deployed oauth2-proxy config and systemd unit state.
